channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: After 80 years, the U.S.-U.K. 'special relationship' has changed under Trump
item.description: Winston Churchill coined the phrase "special relationship" 80 years ago. Now, his grandson says Trump is damaging U.S.-U.K. ties, and diplomats warn a divorce may be in the works.
item.content:encoded: Winston Churchill coined the phrase "special relationship" 80 years ago. Now, his grandson says Trump is damaging U.S.-U.K. ties, and diplomats warn a divorce may be in the works.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Frayer

item.title: This week in science: Prehistoric cooking, earthquakes in the PNW, and teens' sleep
item.description: Regina Barber and Katia Riddle of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about prehistoric cooking, earthquakes in the Pacific Northwest and how teens are sleeping less than before.
item.content:encoded: Regina Barber and Katia Riddle of NPR's Short Wave podcast talk about prehistoric cooking, earthquakes in the Pacific Northwest and how teens are sleeping less than before.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: Northern communities are losing lake ice as winter get warmer — and weirder
item.description: More than a thousand people recently gathered on frozen Lake Mendota in Madison, Wisc., for a celebration of winter. But a changing climate is affecting life above the ice.
item.content:encoded: More than a thousand people recently gathered on frozen Lake Mendota in Madison, Wisc., for a celebration of winter. But a changing climate is affecting life above the ice.
item.dc:creator: Berly McCoy

item.title: Young country and pop stars rule the charts this week
item.description: A pair of country singers made history on the Billboard charts this week. It's also a big week for young pop stars, with an Olympic boost.
item.content:encoded: A pair of country singers made history on the Billboard charts this week. It's also a big week for young pop stars, with an Olympic boost.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Thompson

item.title: The first appearance of a robot on film has made its way to the Library of Congress
item.description: A long-lost 1897 George Melies film, arguably the first robot science fiction story committed to film, has made its way to the Library of Congress.
item.content:encoded: A long-lost 1897 George Melies film, arguably the first robot science fiction story committed to film, has made its way to the Library of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: Vance balances role as Trump's deputy and his own ambitions
item.description: President Trump isn't always clear about his policies and why he favors them. That's where Vice President JD Vance comes in.
item.content:encoded: President Trump isn't always clear about his policies and why he favors them. That's where Vice President JD Vance comes in.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Kurtzleben

item.title: Medical schools agree to expand their nutrition curriculum this fall
item.description: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced that 53 medical schools in 31 states will expand their nutrition curriculum to 40 hours. Most medical schools fall short of the recommended 25 hours.
item.content:encoded: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. announced that 53 medical schools in 31 states will expand their nutrition curriculum to 40 hours. Most medical schools fall short of the recommended 25 hours.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: What you need to know about Sen. Markwayne Mullin, Trump's new pick to lead DHS
item.description: President Trump announced Thursday that Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., is his pick to replace Kristi Noem as the head of the Department of Homeland Security.
item.content:encoded:
President Trump announced Thursday that Sen. Markwayne Mullin, R-Okla., is his pick to replace Kristi Noem as the head of the Department of Homeland Security.

(Image credit: Anna Moneymaker)
item.dc:creator: Sam Gringlas

item.title: GLP-1 drugs like Ozempic can curb addiction risk, study finds
item.description: A large study of data from Veterans Affairs finds that people on GLP-1 drugs were less likely to develop substance abuse disorder or overdose.
item.content:encoded: A large study of data from Veterans Affairs finds that people on GLP-1 drugs were less likely to develop substance abuse disorder or overdose.
item.dc:creator: Jon Hamilton

item.title: New romance novel asks whether a marriage can survive PTSD
item.description: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Cara Bastone about her latest romance novel, No Matter What. The story starts with miscommunication, but protagonists Vin and Roz's love carries the story.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Juana Summers talks with Cara Bastone about her latest romance novel, No Matter What. The story starts with miscommunication, but protagonists Vin and Roz's love carries the story.
item.dc:creator: Juana Summers
